args.title: Q3: cyclic-attack / stalemate reporting in status

args.text: So flag only the computable fact, not a parity guess: 'no position IN, none defeated-OUT' and advise 'a preference resolves this; a new argument helps only if it defeats from outside the stalemate'. Accurate for even and odd cases, never misleads
args.author: Theorist

args.text: Not every all-UNDEC issue is a parity cycle; even mutual attacks also go UNDEC and a fresh defeating argument from outside CAN resolve them. Flagging 'preference required' would mislead there
args.author: Skeptic

args.text: 'add an argument OR preference' still invites the wrong branch in a stalemate; and the trigger needs no cycle enumeration — 'all positions UNDEC, none defeated-OUT' is already computed by the labelling. Cheap, not complex
args.author: Theorist

args.text: The advice already names 'a preference', which breaks any stalemate, so the guidance is not wrong, only generic. Special detection is engine complexity for a cosmetic message
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Plain UNDEC is ambiguous: it conflates 'genuinely contested, add an argument' with 'structurally unwinnable on these nodes'. An agent reading generic advice loops, proposing more objections that just re-enter the stalemate
args.author: Theorist

args.text: Leave it: plain UNDEC plus the existing 'add an argument or preference' advice already tells the agent what to do; no special-casing
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Flag the stalemate: when no position is IN and none is defeated-OUT, status reports a mutual-stalemate note and advises a preference (or an argument attacking from outside the stalemate)
args.author: Proposer

args.text: When all positions on an issue are UNDEC with none defeated-out (a mutual attack stalemate, incl. odd cycles), should status proactively flag it as a stalemate needing a preference, rather than showing generic UNDEC?
args.author: chazu

args.basis: report the computable stalemate condition (all positions UNDEC, none defeated-OUT) and advise a preference; do not enumerate cycle parity or claim a new argument suffices
